name: Scientific Coalition for UAP Studies
description: A volunteer organization of scientists, engineers, and academics who apply rigorous scientific methodology to the analysis of UAP reports. Best known for the 270-page forensic analysis of the 2004 Nimitz incident and the peer-reviewed Entropy paper on Tic Tac flight characteristics by Kevin Knuth. Publishes technical reports and hosts an annual conference.

name: SOL Foundation
description: A research institute founded to support rigorous scientific and policy investigation of UAP, led by Garry Nolan and Peter Skafish. Hosts an annual symposium at Stanford University featuring scientists, policy professionals, and researchers. Distinguished by its institutional affiliation with Stanford and its emphasis on consciousness research alongside physical analysis.

name: Galileo Project
description: Established by Harvard astrophysicist Avi Loeb following his Oumuamua hypothesis. Operates a multi-sensor observatory in the Harvard Yard to detect and characterize anomalous aerial objects in real-time. Uses a telescope array, infrared cameras, radio receivers, and audio sensors. Takes a purely empirical observational approach distinct from report-analysis models.

name: Bigelow Institute for Consciousness Studies
description: Founded by Robert Bigelow following his sale of Bigelow Aerospace. BICS funds research into consciousness survival after death and anomalous cognition, areas with significant overlap with UAP contact phenomenology. Known for hosting essay competitions with $1M+ in prizes and funding independent researchers globally.

name: J. Allen Hynek Center for UFO Studies
description: Founded by J. Allen Hynek after his break with Project Blue Book. Maintains the UFOCAT cross-referenced database (one of the five sources in DECUR's sightings integration) and publishes the International UFO Reporter. The oldest continuously operating scientific UAP research organization in the United States.

name: National Aviation Reporting Center on Anomalous Phenomena
description: A research organization focused specifically on UAP encounters in commercial and military aviation. Collects reports from pilots and air traffic controllers who cannot file formal reports without career risk. Maintains a database of aviation-specific UAP encounters with safety implications. Works with ICAO on reporting protocol development.

name: Mutual UFO Network
description: The largest civilian UAP investigation organization in the world. Operates a network of trained field investigators who respond to reports, conduct witness interviews, and file structured investigation reports. MUFON reports form one of the five source databases in DECUR's sightings integration. Has investigated hundreds of thousands of cases since 1969.

name: To The Stars Academy of Arts and Science
description: Founded by Tom DeLonge with a board including Luis Elizondo, Hal Puthoff, and Chris Mellon. Responsible for the declassification and release of the Nimitz, Gimbal, and Go Fast videos in 2017-2018. Also produced the History Channel series 'Unidentified.' Effectively catalyzed the current era of government UAP disclosure through strategic media engagement.

name: UAPx
description: An instrumentally-focused research organization that deploys multi-sensor arrays to field locations with high UAP activity, including the ocean near Puerto Rico. Led in part by Tim Gallaudet. Uses calibrated, timestamped sensor data to capture and characterize anomalous events rather than relying on single-sensor or eyewitness testimony alone.

name: Groupe d'Etudes et d'Informations sur les Phenomenes Aerospatiaux Non-identifies
description: The official French government UAP investigation unit, operated under CNES (the French space agency). One of the oldest continuously operating official UAP investigation programs in the world. Publishes its case database publicly. Categorizes reports into four types (A through D) based on explanatory confidence. Highly regarded for transparency and methodological rigor.

name: Comite de Estudios de Fenomenos Aereos Anomalos
description: Chile's official UAP investigation committee, operating under the Directorate General of Civil Aeronautics. Investigates reports from pilots, air traffic controllers, and military personnel. Released the El Bosque Air Base video in 2014 after a 2-year forensic analysis - one of the most transparent official UAP case releases by any government.

name: All-domain Anomaly Resolution Office
description: The U.S. Department of Defense's official UAP investigation office, established by the National Defense Authorization Act for FY2022. Succeeded the UAP Task Force. Responsible for detecting, identifying, and attributing UAP encounters across all domains - air, space, sea, and subsurface. Maintains a secure reporting mechanism for military and intelligence personnel.

name: National Aeronautics and Space Administration
description: NASA established a UAP research function in 2023 following the UAP Independent Study Team report. Appointed Mark McInerney as Director of UAP Research. Focused on applying NASA's existing sensor assets and data science capabilities to UAP detection and characterization. Represents the first time NASA has formally engaged with the UAP topic institutionally.

name: UAP Disclosure Fund
description: A non-profit advocacy organization that funds legal representation for whistleblowers and lobbies Congress for stronger UAP transparency legislation. Supported the legal proceedings around David Grusch's whistleblower case. Works with the attorneys involved in the National Security Act whistleblower protections that enabled congressional UAP testimony.

name: The Black Vault
description: The largest online FOIA document archive, operated by John Greenewald Jr. since 1996. Has filed over 10,000 FOIA requests and hosts hundreds of thousands of declassified government documents. An essential primary source repository for UAP researchers working with declassified materials. Responsible for obtaining the full AATIP program documents and other key releases.
